pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates file at /data/uploads/critical_manifest.json (line 43); export_result.sh verifies path as /app/data/uploads/critical_manifest.json (line 16) when mounted to /app/data
setup_task.sh references 'recovery-service' for cleanup (line 21); export_result.sh checks CONTAINER_NAME='recovery-service' (line 7); task description explicitly names the container
verifier.py line 90 confirms passed = (score >= 100); scoring breakdown: container running 20pts + file exists 40pts + content match 40pts = 100pts total

privileged_info_summary: The docker_orphaned_volume_rescue task requires an agent to locate a specific orphaned Docker volume containing critical_manifest.json and mount it to a new container named recovery-service at path /app/data. The target file path is /app/data/uploads/critical_manifest.json. The task uses synthetic test data generated with a unique UUID during setup. The pass threshold for task completion is 100 points, achieved by: container running (20pts), file existing in correct location (40pts), and content verification matching the setup-generated unique ID (40pts).
